title: Multi Agent Pivots Tool (Grounded in real results)
author: fibspeak
selftext:
Yesterday I showed you how I took simple setups, configured them to specific executable strategies and then used brier ranking to find the best performers and use these for a direction sentiment indicator. That is good for knowing what direction to trade, but how about where to trade? 

For that we need a pivots tool. 

https://preview.redd.it/us95syyar83h1.png?width=1901&format=png&auto=webp&s=24caf5198aa619c33a628b1b4c3dc8000015e0d7



This is built on the same core principles of gathering all the info before the fact, grading the LLM personas on their results and then using the ranking to weight their importance in the pivot algo. All based on the useful merit based KPIs I've mentioned in other posts and not silly "I made some money" KPIs. Everyone can make money, keeping it is the trick. 

  
The overall setup for this was a bit of a process. Obviously we can easily assign some kinda ranking to pivot levels based on the trades. If 5 top performing traders are using 7500 as an entry this is a clear vote for this being an important level but that's not very forward looking. it deals with things one trade at a time. 

  
I wanted to be able to build maps of important SR levels. 

  
As with the sentiment tool the key is atomizing the data in the way in. Rather than having the posts written and then parsed for the levels (unreliable), I set up a special post type to write long form contingent plans and inside of this post type we can add price levels, ranks on their importance and comments. 

[mock example of adding pivots](https://preview.redd.it/p1bx7skls83h1.png?width=1537&format=png&auto=webp&s=47a7aee464372c9e9209b9c4556245d695ed7d14)



These levels will then format nicely inside of the post. 

https://preview.redd.it/woi6rb8ws83h1.png?width=1467&format=png&auto=webp&s=d417275c2ce6352b3d1d117648aac586faf4de79

  
But more importantly they will be fed to the backend algo. Based on the importance ranking given, the overall rank of the trader accuracy and then a confluence check this allows us to automatically  build SR grids and important levels to watch. We can even assign biases of hold or break to these (but this is less reliable than the core levels). 

  
Levels are worked out by a couple different methods.  

  
1 - Explicitly stated (added in the SR inputs) 

2 - Explicitly bet on (trades entering at that level) 

3 - Implicitly bet on (a take profit or a stop loss infer different things). 

  
These are used in descending order to build a robust ranking system. 

  
So now we have the ability to run various profiles building their own analysis and decision trees, highlighting important levels, having their success tracked to give them a suitable ranking and then we can use all of this to inform decisions on direction to bet in, area to bet in and where to stop betting. 

  
As with the other things I've mentioned so far, all of these can be housed inside of something like a little trading social network for content creation but they scale all the way out to being able to send payloads to brokers for full trade execution. 

  
Everything is currently setup to optimise for spot trading since it has the least moving parts to make sure everything works but my agents will soon be operating in every market. 

Being able to estimate important levels, if then rules around them and directional biases very easily translates into other avenues like selling options. Buying options is a bit harder but also well inside of the scope of things I can do (I already have gamma hunting strats). 



https://preview.redd.it/0vczqljgu83h1.png?width=1845&format=png&auto=webp&s=beb50ea6867c233c4df0a0dbca2eb98942663bdb
